title: Tavita's Touch
intro: Tavita's ceramics are precise in a way you rarely see by hand. The brand had to show that.

blocks.heading: The problem
blocks.text: Handmade ceramics almost always look the same — earthy, rustic, "cozy." Tavita's work isn't cozy. It's controlled.

blocks.heading: The anchor
blocks.text: Tavitaʼs perfectionism at the wheel became the brand's core truth: craft, taken further than craft usually goes.

blocks.heading: The mark
blocks.text: Two T's, meeting like hands closing around clay. One continuous line — handmade in spirit, exact in form.

blocks.heading: Typography
blocks.text: A modern serif, thin weight, lowercase. Thin lines don't forgive mistakes — that's the point.

blocks.heading: Color
blocks.text: Warm charcoal and cream, pulled from the clay itself. No bright color. Restraint reads as expensive.
